name: toggle-group
description: A set of toggle buttons with single- or multiple-selection; the selected value(s) are server state. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the selection lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-toggle-group> Lit island. Radix ToggleGroup a11y: single = radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple = group > button[aria-pressed].
docs: Register ToggleGroupComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `values` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `icons` (index-aligned, '' = none) and `disabledValues`, plus `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') and the whole-group `disabled`, as locked server config; the buttons render themselves. Each item toggles with `l:click="$set('toggleValue','<value>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the selected set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side; single mode keeps at most one and allows deselect-to-empty, Radix behaviour). Single mode renders role=radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple renders role=group > button[aria-pressed]. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/toggle-group")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).
